The storm triggered red rainstorm alerts in parts of China and continued to cause flooding and infrastructure damage.

Response efforts are hampered by funding shortages, unpaid health workers, and the closure of USAID, while new diagnostic tests and treatment trials offer hope.

logs.summary: A wildfire erupted in the historic Fontainebleau forest south of Paris, burning over 2,000 hectares and prompting the evacuation of about 1,000 residents. French authorities arrested two people suspected of deliberately starting the blaze, while hundreds of firefighters and waterbombing aircraft worked to contain the fire as Europe faced another heatwave.

logs.summary: The wildfires in the Fontainebleau forest have shown mixed status: some reports indicate containment, while others report the fire is still spreading with 850 firefighters and 200 vehicles deployed. Authorities discovered ten ignition points and arrested two suspects on suspicion of arson.

logs.summary: A fast-moving wildfire in southern Spain's Almería province, starting around July 9-10, 2026, killed at least 13 people and left over 20 missing, many of them foreign nationals (including British and Belgian). The fire, which prompted evacuations of roughly 800 people, is suspected to have been ignited by a broken power line amid a severe summer heatwave. It is considered one of Spain's deadliest wildfires on record.
